title: PTY session manager with WebSocket attachment and terminal process control
purpose: Manages in-memory PTY sessions using Bun's native terminal API, handling process creation, input/output streaming, WebSocket attachment, and session lifecycle management.
module_context: Exports a singleton PTYManager instance and interfaces for session management including PTYSession, PTYSessionInfo, CreateOptions, and AttachOptions.

steps.content: IF a shell is explicitly requested, validate its existence and return it.
ELSE check the environment for SHELL variable and validate its existence.
IF SHELL is not available or invalid, try fallback shells in order: zsh, bash, sh.
IF no valid shell is found among all options, throw an error indicating no shell is available.
Validate that the session ID is not already in use.
IF the session ID is invalid, throw an error.
Determine the shell to use based on options or environment.
Set the working directory from options or default to current working directory.
Set terminal dimensions from options or use defaults.
Initialize a new session object with the determined values.
Spawn a new subprocess with the determined shell and terminal settings.
Configure data and exit callbacks for the terminal to handle output and process termination.
Store the spawned process and terminal reference in the session.
Set up process exit handling to clean up session on process exit.
Store the session in the internal sessions map.
Return session information including ID, shell, working directory, creation time, last activity, and client count.
Look up the session by ID in the internal sessions map.
IF the session does not exist, throw an error.
Write the input data to the terminal associated with the session.
IF writing fails, log a warning but continue execution.
Update the last activity timestamp for the session.

methods.name: resize
methods.normalized_params: (sessionId: string, cols: number, rows: number): void

steps.content: Look up the session by ID in the internal sessions map.
IF the session does not exist, return without action.
Resize the terminal associated with the session to the specified dimensions.
IF resizing fails, log a warning but continue execution.
Track whether this is the first resize received for the session.
Mark the session as having received a resize.
IF this was the first resize and replay is deferred, replay the buffer to connected websockets.
Update the last activity timestamp for the session.

methods.name: attach
methods.normalized_params: (sessionId: string, ws: ServerWebSocket<any>, options?: AttachOptions): void

steps.content: Look up the session by ID in the internal sessions map.
IF the session does not exist, create a new one with default settings.
Add the WebSocket connection to the session's set of connected websockets.
Store the deferReplay option from the attach options.
IF replay is not deferred, replay the buffer contents to the newly attached WebSocket.
Update the last activity timestamp for the session.
Look up the session by ID in the internal sessions map.
IF the session does not exist, return without action.
Retrieve the contents of the session's buffer.
IF buffer contents exist, send them to the specified WebSocket.
IF sending fails, log a warning but continue execution.
Look up the session by ID in the internal sessions map.
IF the session does not exist, return without action.
Remove the WebSocket connection from the session's set of connected websockets.
Look up the session by ID in the internal sessions map.
IF the session does not exist, return without action.
Broadcast an exit message to all connected websockets for the session.
Close all WebSocket connections associated with the session.
Close the terminal associated with the session.
Kill the subprocess associated with the session.
Clear the session's buffer.
Remove the session from the internal sessions map.
Initialize an empty result array for session information.
Iterate through all sessions in the internal sessions map.
For each session, construct and push session information to the result array.
Return the array of session information.
Check if a session with the given ID exists in the internal sessions map.
Return true if the session exists, false otherwise.
Look up the session by ID in the internal sessions map.
IF the session does not exist, return undefined.
Construct and return session information including ID, shell, working directory, creation time, last activity, and client count.
Iterate through all session IDs in the internal sessions map.
For each session ID, call the kill method to terminate and clean up the session.
